User Story: As a private journal owner, I want protected pages to redirect me to login when I am unauthenticated so my journal stays private.
Expected Behavior: Unauthenticated page requests redirect to /login with next set to the originally requested path unless public read is enabled.
Errors Found: Not browser-tested with real cookies because no JOURNAL_ACCESS_TOKEN/Supabase test environment was provided.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to enter my access token so I can open the app.
Expected Behavior: Submitting a valid token to /api/login sets an httpOnly cookie for one year and routes to the next path; invalid tokens show an error and keep me on the login page.
Errors Found: Not integration-tested against a real JOURNAL_ACCESS_TOKEN in this environment.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to change token/logout so another token can be entered.
Expected Behavior: Clicking Change token POSTs /api/logout, clears the auth cookie, and sends the browser to /login.
Errors Found: Not browser-tested with real cookies because no auth env was provided.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a journaler, I want to type today's note and save it with Enter or Ctrl/Cmd+S so today's journal entry is recorded quickly.
Expected Behavior: A non-empty trimmed draft upserts today's UTC entry, reloads the full entry list, recomputes streak state, focuses/selects the input, and unlocks the daily quote.
Errors Found: Save button looked actionable for empty drafts even though empty saves are ignored, which is a UX mismatch.

User Story: As a journaler, I want to edit previous entries inline so I can correct a day without opening a separate screen.
Expected Behavior: Blurring an edited row saves trimmed non-empty changed text; Enter commits by blurring; failed saves revert visible text.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a journaler, I want to delete an entry after confirmation so I can remove mistakes.
Expected Behavior: Clicking Delete prompts for confirmation, calls DELETE /api/entries/:day, and reloads the full entry list.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a journaler, I want to search previous entries so I can find memories by text.
Expected Behavior: Typing in search debounces a GET /api/entries?q=... request; results are newest first and case-insensitive via ilike.
Errors Found: The / keyboard shortcut intercepted slash while focus was already in editable fields such as search, making slash input awkward.

User Story: As a keyboard-oriented user, I want shortcuts for save and search so common actions are fast.
Expected Behavior: Ctrl/Cmd+S saves the draft; / focuses search only when focus is not already in an input, textarea, select, button, or contenteditable element.
Errors Found: Shortcut handling was too broad for / before the fix.

User Story: As a user, I want to export all entries to JSON so I can back up my journal.
Expected Behavior: Clicking Export downloads verum-export.json from /api/entries/export, ordered oldest first and formatted as JSON.
Errors Found: Not browser-download-tested because no running authenticated Supabase environment was provided.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to see my current streak so I know whether I have logged today.
Expected Behavior: The app computes current streak, best streak support, logged-today state, and renders current days plus Logged/Not logged today.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found. API endpoint exists but UI computes from entries instead of fetching /api/streak.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want a quote after logging today so journaling is rewarded by a daily prompt.
Expected Behavior: QuoteBox appears only after today's entry exists or is saved; it fetches /api/quotes/daily and handles no-quote/loading states.
Errors Found: Not integration-tested because the Supabase RPC verum_pick_daily_quote is not available locally.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to thumbs up/down a quote so the app can learn core quotes.
Expected Behavior: Thumb buttons POST delta 1 or -1, update score server-side, and return quote with is_core based on threshold.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to delete a quote after confirmation so unwanted quotes stop appearing.
Expected Behavior: Delete prompts for confirmation, deletes the quote, relies on daily-pick FK cascade, and refreshes the daily quote.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As an integrator, I want quote list/create endpoints so quotes can be curated outside the UI.
Expected Behavior: GET /api/quotes lists all/core/regular quotes; POST /api/quotes creates idempotently by text+author and returns is_core.
Errors Found: No visible UI exists for adding quotes despite empty-state copy saying add via API; acceptable based on current code/API scope.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want a life-calendar visualization so I can see elapsed weeks and weeks with entries.
Expected Behavior: The calendar renders configured lifespan rows, marks past/current/compressed weeks, and adds dots for entry weeks.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As an API caller, I want unauthorized writes blocked and optional public reads supported so privacy settings are consistent.
Expected Behavior: API route handlers call requireAuth; GETs are public only when JOURNAL_PUBLIC_READ=true; mutations require the token cookie.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a maintainer, I want non-interactive validation commands so CI/agents can verify the app without prompts.
Expected Behavior: npm run lint should complete non-interactively in this repo instead of opening Next's ESLint setup prompt.
Errors Found: next lint prompts for ESLint configuration in this project, which is a logistical testing error in non-interactive environments.
